_metadata.description: Standalone medication profile-gate rules for stack medications. Rules use the same v6.0 profile_gate schema as ingredient interaction warnings, but the product context is a stack medication class.

change_log.change: Add kidney disease NSAID medication-profile rule for stack medication review.
Add pregnancy NSAID medication-profile rule for stack medication review.

medication_profile_gate_rules.headline: Review NSAID use in pregnancy
medication_profile_gate_rules.body: NSAIDs such as ibuprofen and naproxen are generally avoided from 20 weeks of pregnancy; after about 30 weeks, they should not be used unless your clinician specifically directs.
medication_profile_gate_rules.management: Check with your OB/clinician before using NSAIDs during pregnancy. Acetaminophen may be discussed as an option, but use should still follow clinician guidance.

medication_profile_gate_rules.headline: NSAIDs are not recommended with kidney disease
medication_profile_gate_rules.body: NSAIDs such as ibuprofen and naproxen can reduce kidney blood flow and may worsen kidney function, especially with existing kidney disease, dehydration, low blood pressure, longer use, or advanced kidney disease.
medication_profile_gate_rules.management: Avoid NSAID use unless your kidney clinician or prescriber specifically directs it. Ask about alternatives and monitoring if pain or inflammation treatment is needed.
